title: Burn down remaining Test262 language failures and skips to 100%
description:
Own the full raw Test262 `language` gap for Lyng JS, including both in-scope ECMA-262 failures and currently skipped proposal coverage. This epic tracks the remaining work needed to move the language slice from the April 23, 2026 baseline to zero unexplained failures and zero skips.

Baseline evidence:
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language --report /tmp/lyng-js-language.md -j 4` produced 23,637 selected, 22,768 runnable, 22,005 passed, 763 failed, 869 skipped.
- A repeat `--list-failures` run reported 761 failures, so there is minor run-to-run variance; treat the April 23, 2026 /tmp artifacts as the analysis source of truth.

This epic intentionally includes proposal-driven skips under `language`, not just Phase 6 core semantics, because the goal is to clear the raw language pass-rate gap to 100%.
acceptance:
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language --report /tmp/lyng-js-language.md -j 4` reports `Failed: 0`, `Skipped: 0`, and `Panicked: 0`.
- Child issues cover the main failure and skip clusters with explicit acceptance criteria and relevant verification commands.
- Tracker verification via `dcat list --agent-only` and `dcat list <epic-id>` shows one epic and the full child breakdown.
notes: Use the /tmp April 23, 2026 language artifacts and the checked-in Test262 harness/reporting flow as the baseline. No dependencies should be added between child issues unless implementation sequencing later proves necessary.

title: Close language optional-chaining, super, and delete/spread lowering gaps
description:
Own the Test262 `language` failures caused by unsupported expression lowering for optional chaining, `super` call/spread forms, and `delete` with `super` property references. The current cluster is centered on `UnsupportedExpression` failures across `language/expressions/optional-chaining`, `language/expressions/super`, `language/expressions/delete/super-property*`, and the private-field optional-chain cases under class elements.

Representative failures:
- `language/expressions/optional-chaining/call-expression.js`
- `language/expressions/super/call-spread-mult-iter.js`
- `language/statements/class/elements/private-field-after-optional-chain.js`

Relevant paths:
- `crates/lyng-js/compiler/src`
- `crates/lyng-js/vm/src`
- `crates/lyng-js/tests/src`
acceptance:
- Owned Test262 slices no longer report `UnsupportedExpression` lowering failures.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/expressions/optional-chaining --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-optional-chaining.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/expressions/super --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-super.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/expressions/delete --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-delete.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- Class-element optional-chain regressions are covered by targeted tests, and `cargo test -p lyng-js-compiler -p lyng-js-vm -p lyng-js-tests` stays green.
notes: Current analysis counted roughly 56 failures in this cluster. This issue owns lowering and runtime semantics for optional chaining, `super` spread/call forms, and `delete super.prop`; computed-property key coercion stays with the class/object issue.

title: Close language destructuring and rest-pattern lowering gaps
description:
Own the Test262 `language` failures caused by unsupported destructuring and rest-pattern lowering in the compiler/VM pipeline. The current cluster is centered on `UnsupportedPattern` failures across `language/statements/try/dstr`, `language/statements/for-of/dstr`, `language/expressions/assignment/dstr`, `language/expressions/assignment/destructuring`, `language/rest-parameters`, and related catch-binding or parameter-pattern cases.

Representative failures:
- `language/statements/try/dstr/ary-init-iter-get-err.js`
- `language/expressions/assignment/dstr/array-rest-iter-rtrn-close.js`
- `language/rest-parameters/object-pattern.js`

Relevant paths:
- `crates/lyng-js/compiler/src`
- `crates/lyng-js/vm/src`
- `crates/lyng-js/tests/src`
- `tools/lyng-js-test262/src`
acceptance:
- Owned Test262 slices no longer report `UnsupportedPattern` failures.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/statements/try/dstr --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-try-dstr.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/expressions/assignment/dstr --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-assignment-dstr.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/rest-parameters --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-rest-parameters.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- `cargo test -p lyng-js-compiler`, `cargo test -p lyng-js-vm`, and relevant `cargo test -p lyng-js-tests` coverage stay green.
notes: Current analysis counted roughly 106 failures in this cluster, heavily concentrated in `try` destructuring plus parameter/rest pattern variants. Keep this issue scoped to lowering and runtime handling for destructuring/rest patterns; non-pattern statement-completion tails belong elsewhere.

title: Close language class, object, and computed-property key gaps
description:
Own the Test262 `language` failures caused by missing or incorrect computed-property key handling for classes, object literals, and namespace/object-related surfaces. The current cluster is centered on `UnsupportedPropertyKey` failures across `language/statements/class`, `language/expressions/class`, `language/computed-property-names`, `language/expressions/object`, and `language/module-code/namespace/internals`.

Representative failures:
- `language/statements/class/cpn-class-decl-fields-methods-computed-property-name-from-arrow-function-expression.js`
- `language/expressions/class/elements/computed-name-toprimitive.js`
- `language/expressions/object/literal-property-name-bigint.js`

Relevant paths:
- `crates/lyng-js/compiler/src`
- `crates/lyng-js/vm/src`
- `crates/lyng-js/objects/src`
- `crates/lyng-js/tests/src`
acceptance:
- Owned Test262 slices no longer report `UnsupportedPropertyKey` failures.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/statements/class --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-statements-class.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/expressions/class --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-expressions-class.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/computed-property-names --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-computed-property-names.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/expressions/object --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-expressions-object.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- Owning-layer tests cover computed-name coercion and private/class element semantics, and `cargo test -p lyng-js-compiler -p lyng-js-vm -p lyng-js-objects -p lyng-js-tests` stays green.
notes: Current analysis counted roughly 55 failures in this cluster. Keep the scope on property-key coercion, computed-name evaluation ordering, and class/object literal semantics rather than optional-chaining or spread lowering.

title: Fix language statement completion-value and lexical-cleanup exactness
description:
Own the remaining non-destructuring statement completion-value and lexical-cleanup failures in the Test262 `language` slice. This covers `language/statements/try` excluding the `*/dstr/*` lowering cluster, plus `language/statements/switch`, `language/statements/if`, `language/statements/while`, `language/statements/do-while`, `language/statements/return`, and remaining lexical-open/cleanup cases not owned by the destructuring issue.

Representative failures:
- `language/statements/try/completion-values.js`
- `language/statements/switch/cptn-dflt-final.js`
- `language/statements/if/cptn-else-false-abrupt-empty.js`

Relevant paths:
- `crates/lyng-js/compiler/src`
- `crates/lyng-js/vm/src`
- `crates/lyng-js/tests/src`
acceptance:
- Owned Test262 statement slices pass with correct completion-value and lexical-cleanup behavior.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/statements/try/cptn --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-try-cptn.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/statements/switch --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-switch.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/statements/if --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-if.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- `cargo test -p lyng-js-vm -p lyng-js-tests` stays green, with direct tests covering completion propagation and lexical cleanup.
notes: Current analysis showed this tail concentrated in `try`, `switch`, `if`, loop completion variants, and lexical-open cases. Destructuring-pattern lowering in `try`/`for-of` is explicitly out of scope for this issue and belongs to the dedicated lowering task.

title: Fix language dynamic-scope, eval, arguments, with, and tail-call exactness
description:
Own the Test262 `language` failures around dynamic scope and caller-sensitive execution semantics, especially `arguments` objects, `with` / `@@unscopables`, direct `eval` strictness and scope integration, directive-prologue strictness, and proper-tail-call behavior. This cluster also includes the current stack-overflow failures in the TCO call tests.

Representative failures:
- `language/arguments-object/10.6-12-2.js`
- `language/expressions/arrow-function/unscopables-with.js`
- `language/expressions/call/tco-non-eval-function.js`

Relevant paths:
- `crates/lyng-js/env/src`
- `crates/lyng-js/vm/src`
- `crates/lyng-js/ops/src`
- `crates/lyng-js/tests/src`
- `docs/lyng-js/dynamic-scope-and-eval.md`
acceptance:
- Owned Test262 slices no longer fail on `arguments` exactness, `with` / `@@unscopables`, directive-prologue strictness, or TCO stack-overflow exits.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/arguments-object --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-arguments-object.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/directive-prologue --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-directive-prologue.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/expressions/call --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-expressions-call.md -j 4` no longer shows worker stack-overflow failures in the owned TCO cases.
- `cargo test -p lyng-js-env -p lyng-js-vm -p lyng-js-tests` stays green.
notes: This issue owns the semantic tail called out in the Phase 6 `6H` docs: dynamic scope, `eval`, lexical spillover, `arguments` exactness, and proper tail calls. Keep pure statement-completion tails in the separate statement exactness issue.

title: Fix language BigInt, reference, and operator exactness
description:
Own the Test262 `language` failures caused by incorrect BigInt coercion, reference handling, operator semantics, and abrupt-completion propagation. The current cluster is centered on `abrupt completion` failures plus adjacent exactness tails across addition, bitwise operators, shifts, comparisons, unary operators, compound assignments, increment/decrement, `instanceof`, `typeof`, and related primitive/object coercion behavior.

Representative failures:
- `language/expressions/bitwise-and/bigint.js`
- `language/expressions/less-than/bigint-and-number.js`
- `language/expressions/compound-assignment/S11.13.2_A7.10_T4.js`

Relevant paths:
- `crates/lyng-js/ops/src`
- `crates/lyng-js/vm/src`
- `crates/lyng-js/tests/src`
acceptance:
- Owned Test262 slices no longer report `abrupt completion` failures or related operator exactness regressions.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/expressions/bitwise-and --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-bitwise-and.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/expressions/less-than --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-less-than.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/expressions/compound-assignment --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-compound-assignment.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- Owning-layer tests cover `ToPrimitive`, `ToNumeric`, comparison ordering, and abrupt-completion propagation, and `cargo test -p lyng-js-vm -p lyng-js-tests` stays green.
notes: Current analysis counted roughly 54 explicit `abrupt completion` failures and a broader operator exactness tail nearby. Keep this issue focused on semantic exactness in `ops`/`vm`, not parser or lowering-only gaps.

title: Implement remaining skipped language proposal and harness coverage
description:
Own the rest of the currently skipped Test262 `language` coverage that is outside the module-proposal bucket but still counts against the raw language pass rate. This includes `regexp-modifiers` (83), `decorators` (24), `raw harness tests are deferred` (29), `resizable-arraybuffer` (7), and `immutable-arraybuffer` (5), for roughly 148 skipped tests in the current baseline.

Relevant paths:
- `crates/lyng-js/parser/src`
- `crates/lyng-js/compiler/src`
- `crates/lyng-js/vm/src`
- `tools/lyng-js-test262/src`

This task also owns the harness-side work needed to stop deferring the remaining raw harness tests under the `language` slice.
acceptance:
- The `language` slice no longer reports skips for `regexp-modifiers`, `decorators`, `raw harness tests are deferred`, `resizable-arraybuffer`, or `immutable-arraybuffer`.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language --report /tmp/lyng-js-language.md -j 4` shows those skip reasons at `0`.
- Relevant parser/compiler/vm/harness tests are added or updated, and `cargo test -p lyng-js-parser -p lyng-js-compiler -p lyng-js-vm -p lyng-js-tests` stays green.
notes: This task owns the remaining proposal and harness skip reasons after the module-proposal task. If one of these proposals turns out to be much larger than expected, split it into a follow-on child task rather than overloading unrelated runtime fixes.

title: Eliminate language parser, lexer, and timeout tails
description:
Own the remaining Test262 `language` frontend and performance tails that are still showing up as parser/lexer strictness gaps or timeouts at the default 1.0s limit. This includes the current identifier timeout cluster plus remaining literal and syntax-edge failures across `language/identifiers`, `language/literals/regexp`, `language/literals/string`, `language/comments`, `language/statements/let`, `language/statements/const`, and `language/statements/for-of/head-lhs-async-invalid.js`.

Representative failures:
- `language/identifiers/start-unicode-17.0.0-class.js`
- `language/literals/regexp/...`
- `language/statements/for-of/head-lhs-async-invalid.js`

Relevant paths:
- `crates/lyng-js/lexer/src`
- `crates/lyng-js/parser/src`
- `crates/lyng-js/tests/src`
- `tools/lyng-js-test262/src`
acceptance:
- Owned language slices no longer time out at the default `1.0s` timeout and no longer show the owned frontend failures.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/identifiers --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-identifiers.md -j 4` completes without the current timeout tail.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/literals/regexp --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-regexp-literals.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/statements/for-of/head-lhs-async-invalid.js --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-for-of-head-lhs-async-invalid.md -j 4` reports the expected parse behavior.
- `cargo test -p lyng-js-lexer -p lyng-js-parser -p lyng-js-tests` stays green.
notes: Current analysis counted 19 timeout failures, concentrated in Unicode identifier tests, plus a small parser/lexer strictness tail. Keep this issue focused on frontend correctness and performance, not runtime semantic exactness.

This includes the skip reasons `deferred to async module milestones` (269), `source-phase-imports` (219), `import-defer` (214), `json-modules` (13), and `import-text` (6), for a total of roughly 721 skipped tests in the current baseline.

notes: The current baseline report shows these module-proposal skips as the single largest skipped-language bucket. If the implementation needs to split into additional follow-on issues later, keep them parented here or reparent them under this task after discussion.

title: Implement skipped language module-proposal coverage
Close language optional-chaining, super, and delete/spread lowering gaps
description:
Own the Test262 `language` failures caused by unsupported expression lowering for optional chaining, `super` call/spread forms, and `delete` with `super` property references. The current cluster is centered on `UnsupportedExpression` failures across `language/expressions/optional-chaining`, `language/expressions/super`, `language/expressions/delete/super-property*`, and the private-field optional-chain cases under class elements.

Representative failures:
- `language/expressions/optional-chaining/call-expression.js`
- `language/expressions/super/call-spread-mult-iter.js`
- `language/statements/class/elements/private-field-after-optional-chain.js`

Relevant paths:
- `crates/lyng-js/compiler/src`
- `crates/lyng-js/vm/src`
- `crates/lyng-js/tests/src`
acceptance:
- Owned Test262 slices no longer report `UnsupportedExpression` lowering failures.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/expressions/optional-chaining --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-optional-chaining.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/expressions/super --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-super.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language/expressions/delete --report /tmp/lyng-js-language-delete.md -j 4` passes its owned cases.
- Class-element optional-chain regressions are covered by targeted tests, and `cargo test -p lyng-js-compiler -p lyng-js-vm -p lyng-js-tests` stays green.
notes: Current analysis counted roughly 56 failures in this cluster. This issue owns lowering and runtime semantics for optional chaining, `super` spread/call forms, and `delete super.prop`; computed-property key coercion stays with the class/object issue.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Research finding for Test262 proposal-stage policy: local Test262 metadata exposes feature flags but no numeric proposal stage. TC39 active/finished proposal lists should be the stage source of truth. Runner default should include Stage 3+ proposal tests because Stage 3 is recommended for implementation and Test262 accepts Stage 3 proposal coverage. Stage 2.7 proposal tests should be excluded from selection by default and opt-in via --proposal-stage 2.7. Stage 4 implementation gaps should remain visible as normal skips/failures, not hidden as proposal-policy exclusions. Current local Stage 2.7 impact: ShadowRealm 64, immutable-arraybuffer 20, await-dictionary 12, import-bytes 5.
